summary: Add a bounded opt-in AI agent-loop runner that executes scripted MCP-style tool rounds, returns tool results as bounded conversation context, honors cancellation, and stops exactly at the configured round cap.

decisions.decision: Keep the agent runner transport-neutral: the model supplies a text or named tool call, and an injected executor performs one call and returns a serialized tool-result system message; mutation review remains the executor's responsibility.
decisions.rationale: This enables the scripted AC-10.c sequence without coupling the AI module to a desktop process or inventing a second MCP handler owner.

decisions.decision: Increment the round counter before each tool execution and stop immediately when the configured cap is reached; no extra model or tool call occurs after capped state.
decisions.rationale: The cap must bound deliberate looping mocks and protect memory/process stability under N-2.

testEvidence.criterion: The agent loop executes a tool round and caps a deliberately looping model.
testEvidence.test: Run the focused agent-loop and persistence tests once.

red.outcome: RED: no pre-change red test was required for this new runner; tests were written against the exact capped behavior before implementation.

questions: UI opt-in/auto-apply controls, actual MCP handler wiring, persistence hydration/deletion, and hosted AC-10.c remain pending.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
